GameConditionDef.label: whiteout
GameConditionDef.description: A catastrophic global deep freeze that plunges the planet into extreme cold, halting all human travel and trade while forcing the world to survive an extended, dangerous winter.

li.letterText: Atmospheric temperatures are steadily dropping across the planet.\n\nThis is not a standard cold snap. You predict a catastrophic deep freeze is approaching, eventually leading to a complete whiteout. You have a short window to stockpile fuel, harvest your remaining crops, and build heaters before the true cold sets in.

The cold has deepened drastically.\n\nWildlife is fleeing the region, and the flora is dying off. Ensure your colony stays indoors and that your power grid can handle the immense strain of constant heating. The worst is still yet to come.

The whiteout has arrived.

Temperatures have plummeted to unsurvivable levels. Human travel across the planet is now entirely unadvised; you will receive no trade caravans or human raids. However, the bitter cold does not bother mechanoids.

If you persevere, the freeze will eventually break.
The skies are finally clearing and the bitter cold is beginning to recede.\n\nThe whiteout is ending. Temperatures will slowly return to normal over the next quadrum, and life will eventually return.

GameConditionDef.label: Scorch
GameConditionDef.description: A catastrophic global heatwave that plunges the planet into extreme heat, halting all outdoor farming and travel while forcing the world to survive an extended, dangerous summer.

li.letterText: The planet's atmosphere is beginning to trap immense amounts of solar radiation, bathing the region in an unnatural, oppressive heat.\n\nTravellers warn that this is just the beginning of a massive thermal event. Temperatures will continue to rise drastically. You have a short window to secure your food supply, build coolers, and tailor protective headgear for your colonists before the sun becomes deadly.

The heat has reached dangerous levels, and rain clouds have vanished completely.\n\nOutdoor crops are struggling to grow in the baked earth. Furthermore, the intense UV radiation is causing sun sickness for anyone caught outside without a hat. This sickness makes victims extremely susceptible to heatstroke. Keep your colonists under roofs or ensure they are wearing headgear.

The heat has peaked into a catastrophic inferno. 

A thick, sweltering haze has settled over the area, and outdoor plant growth has completely halted. Worse yet, the superheated atmosphere is generating massive dry thunderstorms. Expect frequent lightning strikes and devastating wildfires.

Stay indoors, keep the coolers running, and wait for the rain to come back.

The oppressive haze is finally lifting, and the sun's glare seems less deadly today.\n\nThe scorch is ending. Temperatures will slowly return to normal over the coming days, and rain clouds will soon return.

GameConditionDef.label: long night
GameConditionDef.description: A catastrophic celestial eclipse that blankets the world in an endless, freezing twilight, forcing the world to adapt to artificial light and increased psychic sensitivity as nocturnal predators and subterranean threats emerge in the darkness.

li.letterText: A space entity of unimaginable size has made first contact with a local star, resulting in the beginning of a long lasting eclipse.\n\nThe daylight is beginning to dim, casting a vast penumbra over the planet. Solar output and temperatures will continue to drop as the eclipse deepens. Prepare your power grids and indoor agriculture before the true darkness arrives.

The celestial entity has begun to cross the center of the sun.\n\nThe planet has been plunged into a deep, freezing twilight. Solar generators are now struggling to produce power, and surface flora is dying. Rely on artificial lights and indoor heating to survive the coming black.

The sun has been completely blotted out. 

Totality has been achieved, and an endless, freezing night has fallen across the world. The celestial entity is emitting a heavy cosmic resonance. Your colonists will tire much faster than normal, and their psychic sensitivity has been dangerously amplified.

The animals became a lot more aggressive. Guard your brightly lit base against the nocturnal predators and subterranean insectoids.

A brilliant flash of light has pierced the horizon!\n\nThe cosmic entity is finally shifting away from the sun's center. Totality has ended. The absolute darkness has broken into twilight. Your solar panels will begin generating power once again, and plants will resume growth as the temperature increases.

The celestial object is making its final pass across the sun.\n\nThe long night is ending. Over the coming days, the remaining shadows will clear, temperatures will normalize, and the planet will fully awaken from the dark.

GameConditionDef.label: psychic bloom
GameConditionDef.description: A dimensional rift tears through the sky, blanketing the world in a perpetual purple aurora while alien seedlings sprout and mutate local flora into psychic lotuses, forcing the world to manage rapidly growing, otherworldly vegetation and intensifying psychic pressure.

li.letterText: The veil between our reality and somewhere else is wearing dangerously thin.\n\nA tear in the dimensional fabric has ignited a permanent, unnatural purple aurora across the sky. Where the psychic energy bleeds through to the surface, bizarre, alien seedlings are manifesting in the soil. While they are harmless for now, they do not belong in this dimension. You may want to uproot them before they anchor themselves to our reality.

The dimensional overlap is widening. The seedlings have matured into psychic lotuses, and they are actively terraforming our world to match their own.

The lotuses are mutating nearby flora into vibrant, otherworldly variants, and the ambient psychic sensitivity of everyone in the region has doubled.

Beware the weather, as clouds from the other side are bleeding through. Psychic rain can have devastating effects on anyone caught outside, as time behaves differently in its home dimension.

The boundary between realities has completely collapsed!

The otherworldly flora has entered a catastrophic superbloom, spreading at an uncontrollable rate. Worse, the psychic lotuses are acting as conduits for the crushing psychic weight of an entirely different universe. The intensity of this maddening drone is directly tied to how many lotuses are anchoring the rift to your map.

You must sever their connection by cutting down the lotuses immediately, or the sheer psychic pressure of another dimension will drive your colonists insane.

Reality has violently reasserted itself.

The dimensional rift has snapped shut. Cut off from the psychic energy of their home universe, the superbloom has instantly collapsed into a hideous, withered wasteland. The maddening drone is gone, but the dead flora will take years to decay naturally. You will need to burn or chop away these wilted plants to make room for your crops to return.

Psychic Rain remains a lingering threat until the sky fully heals and the aurora dissipates.

GameConditionDef.label: Psychic Rain
GameConditionDef.description:
A dangerous psychic entity caused disturbance on subatomic levels, resonating across all particles of the atmosphere.
A seemingly endless supernatural rain has begun, altering the very composition of time. Any person or creature not under the roof will age significantly faster.
It will last anywhere between a season to several years.
